title: pediomelum argophyllum
intro: Pediomelum argophyllum, also known as desert peach or western peony, is a flowering plant native to the western United States and Canada. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties. The health benefits of Pediomelum argophyllum include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to make topical creams and ointments for skin care and wound healing.
